title: Homework2
text:
Storing both the pre-treatment and post-treatment readings using the c() function in order to combine the data.

pre_bp <- c(130, 128, 116, 124, 133, 134, 118, 126, 114, 127, 141, 138, 128, 140, 137, 131, 120, 128, 139, 135)

post_bp <- c(114, 98, 113, 99, 107, 116, 113, 111, 119, 117, 101, 119, 130, 122, 106, 106, 124, 102, 117, 113)

Next, we assign names to these vectors by using the paste() function.

patients <- paste("Subject", 1:20, sep = "_")
print(patients)

 [1] "Subject_1"  "Subject_2"  "Subject_3"  "Subject_4"  "Subject_5" 
 [6] "Subject_6"  "Subject_7"  "Subject_8"  "Subject_9"  "Subject_10"
[11] "Subject_11" "Subject_12" "Subject_13" "Subject_14" "Subject_15"
[16] "Subject_16" "Subject_17" "Subject_18" "Subject_19" "Subject_20"


Now we calculate the difference in the measurements by simply subtracting post treatments by pre treatments, and then calculate the average in the blood pressure change.

diff_bp <- post_bp - pre_bp
print(diff_bp)

 [1] -16 -30  -3 -25 -26 -18  -5 -15   5 -10 -40 -19   2 -18 -31 -25   4 -26 -22
[20] -22

### Mean is shown next:
print(mean(diff_bp))

[1] -17


The patients who experienced a decrease in blood pressure after treatment (a positive change) are shown below using the which() function.

positiveChanges <- which(diff_bp > 0, useNames = TRUE)
print(positiveChanges)

[1]  9 13 17





print(mean(positiveChanges))

[1] 13

title: Homework2
text:
Storing both the pre-treatment and post-treatment readings using the c() function in order to combine the data.

pre_bp <- c(130, 128, 116, 124, 133, 134, 118, 126, 114, 127, 141, 138, 128, 140, 137, 131, 120, 128, 139, 135)

post_bp <- c(114, 98, 113, 99, 107, 116, 113, 111, 119, 117, 101, 119, 130, 122, 106, 106, 124, 102, 117, 113)

Next, we assign names to these vectors by using the paste() function.

patients <- paste("Subject", 1:20, sep = "_")
print(patients)

 [1] "Subject_1"  "Subject_2"  "Subject_3"  "Subject_4"  "Subject_5" 
 [6] "Subject_6"  "Subject_7"  "Subject_8"  "Subject_9"  "Subject_10"
[11] "Subject_11" "Subject_12" "Subject_13" "Subject_14" "Subject_15"
[16] "Subject_16" "Subject_17" "Subject_18" "Subject_19" "Subject_20"


Now we calculate the difference in the measurements by simply subtracting post treatments by pre treatments, and then calculate the average in the blood pressure change.

diff_bp <- post_bp - pre_bp
print(diff_bp)

 [1] -16 -30  -3 -25 -26 -18  -5 -15   5 -10 -40 -19   2 -18 -31 -25   4 -26 -22
[20] -22

### Mean is shown next:
print(mean(diff_bp))

[1] -17


The patients who experienced a decrease in blood pressure after treatment (a positive change) are shown below using the which() function.

positiveChanges <- which(diff_bp > 0, useNames = TRUE)
print(positiveChanges)

[1]  9 13 17





print(mean(positiveChanges))

[1] 13

title: Homework2
text:
Task 2:

Task 3:

placebo_patients <- paste("Subject", 1:10, sep = "_")
placebo_pre_treatment <- c(138, 135, 147, 117, 152, 134, 114, 121, 131, 130)
placebo_post_treatment <- c(105, 136, 123, 130, 134, 143, 135, 139, 120, 124)
placebo_diff <- placebo_post_treatment - placebo_pre_treatment
placebo_trial_df <- data.frame(placebo_patients, placebo_pre_treatment, placebo_post_treatment, placebo_diff)

my_list <- list(treatment = treatment_df, placebo = placebo_trial_df)
my_list

$treatment

Next, we assign statuses to each of the rows in the two data frames using control loops and if-else statements

for(index in 1:20) {

  bp <- my_list$treatment$post_bp[index]

  if(bp <= 120) {
    my_list$treatment$status[index] <- "optimal"
  }

  if(bp > 120 & bp <= 130) {
    my_list$treatment$status[index] <- "borderline"
  }

  if(bp > 130) {
    my_list$treatment$status[index] <- "high"
  }
}

print(my_list$treatment)

     patients pre_bp post_bp diff_bp isLessThan     status

for(index in 1:10) {

  bp <- my_list$placebo$placebo_post_treatment[index]

  if(bp <= 120) {
    my_list$placebo$status[index] <- "optimal"
  }

  if(bp > 120 & bp <= 130) {
    my_list$placebo$status[index] <- "borderline"
  }

  if(bp > 130) {
    my_list$placebo$status[index] <- "high"
  }
}

print(my_list$placebo)
